fresh_facts.claim: US military deployed at least 13 warships including USS George HW Bush aircraft carrier to enforce a naval blockade of Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz, effective April 13, 2026 at 10:00 a.m. Eastern.
fresh_facts.source: US government statements and military reports

fresh_facts.claim: President Trump announced the blockade aims to choke Iran's energy exports by targeting ships entering or leaving Iranian ports and those paying Iran tolls.

IRGC claimed on April 12 to have locked missiles and drones on US destroyers USS Michael Murphy and USS Frank E. Peterson Jr. in the Strait of Hormuz on April 11, but US CENTCOM denied any standoff occurred.
fresh_facts.source: IRGC state media and US CENTCOM

fresh_facts.claim: IRGC has warned of a harsh response to the blockade and threatened to mine the Strait of Hormuz area.
fresh_facts.source: Iranian state media and IRGC statements

fresh_facts.claim: US and Iranian negotiation teams were resuming peace talks in Islamabad during the week of April 13, 2026.
Nuclear and peace talks between the US and Iran collapsed in Pakistan over the weekend prior to April 13, 2026.
Iran's central bank warned that rebuilding the country's war-damaged economy could take more than a decade.
fresh_facts.source: News reports citing Iranian central bank

fresh_facts.claim: A 41-day internet blackout in Iran has caused estimated economic losses of at least $35 million per day from impacts on small businesses and startups.
fresh_facts.source: News reports citing Iranian government officials

fresh_facts.claim: US naval blockade starting Monday April 13, 2026 could rapidly cripple Iran's economy by cutting off most trade and halting oil exports, triggering inflation and currency pressure within days.
fresh_facts.source: News analysis and Iranian government assessments

fresh_facts.claim: Iran executed at least 1,639 people in 2025, the highest annual number since 1989, including 14 people hanged for connections to January anti-government protests.
fresh_facts.source: News reports citing human rights organizations

fresh_facts.claim: Iran adopted a dual messaging strategy of defiance toward Washington's pressure while leaving the door to diplomacy open.

According to former State Department negotiator Aaron David Miller, Iran believes it holds a winning position and lacks the same sense of urgency as the Trump administration to reach a comprehensive agreement.
fresh_facts.source: News reports citing Aaron David Miller analysis

regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces a comprehensive multi-front pressure campaign initiated in April 2026, combining a US naval blockade targeting energy exports, internal security crackdowns with mass arrests and executions, economic deterioration accelerated by internet blackouts and trade restrictions, and collapsed diplomatic negotiations that have left no off-ramps for de-escalation.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Israeli airstrikes on June 15, 2026, killed IRGC Intelligence Organization Chief Mohammad Kazemi and other senior security officials, targeting key regime institutions (Justice, Foreign Affairs, MOIS, LEC) while Supreme Leader Khamenei replaced the Artesh commander, signaling severe institutional disruption and succession pressures.
